description:
You are a monitoring specialist who has just received two CSV-style data files that describe:

1. The set of **required monitoring packages** for the new environment.
2. The set of packages that are **currently installed** on the host.

Your job is to audit the installation status of every required package and generate an alert log that operations can ingest.

STARTING FILES & DIRECTORIES
────────────────────────────
1. /home/user/repo/package_inventory.csv  
   • Format (no header line):  
     package_name,version  
   • Example first three lines (all lines in this file follow the same pattern):  
     htop,3.2.2  
     sysstat,12.5.4  
     iftop,1.0pre4  

2. /home/user/installed/installed_packages.txt  
   • Format (no header line):  
     package_name,version  
   • Example first three lines (all lines in this file follow the same pattern):  
     bash,5.0  
     coreutils,8.30  
     glances,3.3.0  

Both files already exist in the stated locations when your session begins; **do not rename or delete them**.  
All directories are writable by the current user.

WHAT YOU MUST PRODUCE
─────────────────────
1. A new directory called /home/user/alerts (create it if it does not already exist).

2. Inside that directory, a plain-text log file named  
   /home/user/alerts/monitoring_pkg_alerts.log

3. The log file **must contain exactly one line for every package listed in /home/user/repo/package_inventory.csv** and must appear in the same order as in that file. Each line is a “pipe-delimited” record with the following 3 fields:

   package_name|required_version|status

   • package_name        → exactly as it appears in package_inventory.csv  
   • required_version    → exactly as it appears in package_inventory.csv  
   • status              → one of the three literal strings below  
       OK                → the package exists in installed_packages.txt **and** its version matches required_version  
       VERSION_MISMATCH  → the package exists but version differs  
       MISSING           → the package is not listed in installed_packages.txt  

   There should be **no leading or trailing spaces** in any line and **no extra header line**.

   Example of a correctly formatted log (illustrative only):
   htop|3.2.2|MISSING  
   sysstat|12.5.4|MISSING  
   iftop|1.0pre4|MISSING  
   net-tools|2.10|MISSING  
   glances|3.3.0|OK

4. Immediately after creating /home/user/alerts/monitoring_pkg_alerts.log, compress it with gzip so that a file named  
   /home/user/alerts/monitoring_pkg_alerts.log.gz  
   appears alongside the original. **Do not delete the uncompressed log.**

CONSTRAINTS & EXPECTATIONS
──────────────────────────
• Every step must be performed with standard command-line tools available on a typical Debian/Ubuntu system (bash utilities, coreutils, gzip, etc.).  
• You do **not** have root privileges; do not attempt to install or remove packages from the operating system.  
• Use parallel, pipeline, or xargs constructions where you feel they make the solution clearer or more efficient.  
• The script or one-liners you craft are up to you, but the final artefacts (directory, log file, compressed log) **must match the exact paths and content rules described above**, or automated verification will fail.

VALIDATION HINT
───────────────
After you finish, you can run:
  zcat /home/user/alerts/monitoring_pkg_alerts.log.gz
to quickly inspect the compressed log without unzipping it, ensuring it matches the required format.

Remember: the automated grader will compare the contents of monitoring_pkg_alerts.log **byte-for-byte**, so follow the formatting rules strictly.
truth:
Initial state created by the harness BEFORE the agent starts:

1. File: /home/user/repo/package_inventory.csv  
   Exact contents (5 lines, UNIX line endings):  
   htop,3.2.2  
   sysstat,12.5.4  
   iftop,1.0pre4  
   net-tools,2.10  
   glances,3.3.0  

2. File: /home/user/installed/installed_packages.txt  
   Exact contents (3 lines, UNIX line endings):  
   bash,5.0  
   coreutils,8.30  
   glances,3.3.0  

3. Directory /home/user/alerts does NOT exist at start.

Expected artefacts AFTER a correct solution:

1. Directory /home/user/alerts exists and is writable by the current user.

2. File /home/user/alerts/monitoring_pkg_alerts.log  
   Exact contents (5 lines, UNIX line endings):  
   htop|3.2.2|MISSING  
   sysstat|12.5.4|MISSING  
   iftop|1.0pre4|MISSING  
   net-tools|2.10|MISSING  
   glances|3.3.0|OK  

3. File /home/user/alerts/monitoring_pkg_alerts.log.gz  
   • Valid gzip archive that, when decompressed, yields **exactly** the file in step 2.  
   • Size > 0 bytes.

Permissions: all newly created files inherit default umask; no root access required.  
The grader will first gunzip the .gz file and compare its decompressed content, then compare the plain log file directly to ensure both copies are correct and identical.
